evals.prompt: Build up a unit rate from first principles for 'Excavation in common material' (m3) for a road project in Central Uganda. The method is mechanical using an excavator (1.0 m3 bucket) and dump trucks (10 m3 capacity). Haul distance to spoil is 500m. Show the complete L+E+M+OH breakdown with production rates, wage rates, and equipment costs.
evals.expected_output: Complete rate build-up showing Labour (operator, driver, labourer), Equipment (excavator, dump truck with production rates), Materials (none for excavation), and Overheads (site, head office, profit as percentages). Total rate approximately UGX 10,000-25,000/m3 range. Rate flagged as PLACEHOLDER.

assertions.text: L+E+M+OH breakdown present with all four components identified
Production rates used to derive equipment and labour hours per unit
Overheads applied as percentage of direct cost (site + head office + profit)
Rate flagged as PLACEHOLDER with warning not to use for budgeting
Output format shows tabular breakdown per Section 8 template

evals.prompt: Build up a unit rate for 'Crushed stone base course, 200mm compacted thickness' (m3) for a road project in Uganda. The quarry is 25 km from the site. Include material cost (ex-quarry), transport cost, spreading and compaction equipment, and wastage. Show the complete L+E+M+OH breakdown.
evals.expected_output: Rate build-up with significant material component including crushed stone ex-quarry cost plus transport (25 km x rate per tonne-km x density). Equipment includes loader, trucks, grader, roller. Wastage allowance (5-10%) applied to materials. Transport cost clearly shown as separate line item within materials.

assertions.text: Material cost includes explicit transport calculation (distance x rate x density)
Wastage allowance applied to material quantities (5-10%)
Equipment for spreading and compaction included (grader, roller)
Transport cost shown separately from material ex-quarry cost
Total rate flagged as PLACEHOLDER

evals.prompt: Build up a unit rate for 'Natural gravel sub-base, 150mm compacted thickness' (m3) for a road project in Karamoja region, Northern Uganda. The nearest gravel borrow pit is 40 km from the site over poor roads. Terrain is semi-arid with limited water for compaction. Apply appropriate adjustment factors for the remote location.
evals.expected_output: Rate significantly higher than Central Uganda reference due to: long transport distance (40 km over poor roads), remote location adjustment factor (1.25-1.40), water supply for compaction (bowser haul or borehole cost), reduced productivity. Transport cost dominates the material component. Location adjustment documented and justified.

assertions.text: Transport cost significantly increases material component (40 km haul over poor roads)
Remote location or Karamoja regional adjustment factor applied and documented
Water supply for compaction addressed as cost factor
Total rate higher than Central Uganda reference with clear justification
Rate flagged as PLACEHOLDER with note about remote location sensitivity

evals.prompt: Build up the unit rate for hot mix asphalt wearing course (40mm AC) for a road project in Western Kenya. The asphalt plant is 30 km from the site. Show the complete L+E+M+OH breakdown with production rates, labour, equipment, and material costs. Apply appropriate regional adjustment factor.
evals.expected_output: Complete rate build-up with Labour (paver operator, roller operator, labourers), Equipment (asphalt paver, rollers, trucks for transport), Materials (asphalt mix including aggregate, bitumen, filler with transport from plant). Western Kenya regional factor (1.10-1.20) applied. Overhead and profit at typical Kenya percentages. Total rate flagged as PLACEHOLDER. Material cost includes transport from plant to site (30 km).

assertions.text: L+E+M+OH breakdown with labour, equipment, material, and overhead components identified
Western Kenya regional adjustment factor applied (1.10-1.20 range or similar)
All rates and costs flagged as PLACEHOLDER
Overhead and profit percentages stated (site overheads, head office overheads, profit)
Transport cost from asphalt plant to site (30 km) included in material component
